---
title: "finding_preregistered_articles"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-24"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Load packages
library(xml2)
library(stringr)
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
```

# Load and filter data key

```{r}
# Load data key (we only need articles from 5 specific journals)
path <- "PsycArticles/data_key (1).xlsx"

data_key <- excel_sheets(path) |>
  purrr::map_dfr(~ read_xlsx(path, sheet = .x))

target_journals <- c(
  "Journal of Experimental Psychology: General",
  "Emotion",
  "Journal of Personality and Social Psychology",
  "Journal of Experimental Psychology: Human Perception and Performance",
  "Journal of Experimental Psychology: Learning, Memory, and Cognition"
)


data_key_filtered <- data_key |>
  filter(`Journal Title` %in% target_journals,
         substr(UID, 1, 4) %in% c("2020", "2021", "2022", "2023", "2024", "2025"))
```

# Unzipping

```{r eval = FALSE}
# only need to do this once, so this code won't run unless you remove eval = FALSE
# unzip the year-batch zips
batch_zips <- list.files("PsycArticles", pattern = "^\\d{4}_\\d+\\.zip$", full.names = TRUE)

for (zip in batch_zips) {
  out_dir <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(zip)
  dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
  unzip(zip, exdir = out_dir)
}

```

```{r eval = FALSE}
# only need to do this once, so this code won't run unless you remove eval = FALSE
# unzip only the UIDs in data_key_filtered
target_uids <- data_key_filtered$UID

# we only want to work out of the zips from 2020 and above 
batch_zips <- list.files("PsycArticles", pattern = "^\\d{4}_\\d+\\.zip$", full.names = TRUE) |>
  Filter(f = \(x) as.integer(substr(basename(x), 1, 4)) >= 2020, x = _)

for (uid in target_uids) {
  matching_zip <- list.files(
    path = batch_zips |> tools::file_path_sans_ext(),
    pattern = paste0("^", uid, "\\.zip$"),
    full.names = TRUE
  )
  
  if (length(matching_zip) == 0) {
    message("Not found: ", uid)
    next
  }
  
  out_dir <- file.path("processed_articles", uid)
  dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
  unzip(matching_zip[1], exdir = out_dir)
}
```


```{r}
article_dirs <- list.dirs("processed_articles", recursive = FALSE)

fulltext_data <- purrr::map_dfr(article_dirs, function(dir) {
  uid <- basename(dir)
  fulltext_file <- list.files(dir, pattern = "_fulltext\\.xml$", full.names = TRUE)
  
  if (length(fulltext_file) == 0) {
    return(tibble(uid = uid, fulltext = NA_character_))
  }
  
  text <- read_xml(fulltext_file[1]) |> xml_text()
  tibble(uid = uid, fulltext = text)
})
```


```{r config}
# --- search for articles that might be preregistered ---

prereg_keywords <- c(
  "preregistered", "pre-registered",
  "preregistration", "pre-registration"
)

extract_context <- function(text, pattern, chars_around = 100) {
  if (is.na(text)) return(list())
  
  matches <- stringr::str_locate_all(text, stringr::regex(pattern, ignore_case = TRUE))[[1]]
  if (nrow(matches) == 0) return(list())
  
  results <- purrr::map(seq_len(nrow(matches)), function(i) {
    start <- max(1, matches[i, "start"] - chars_around)
    end <- min(nchar(text), matches[i, "end"] + chars_around)
    keyword <- substr(text, matches[i, "start"], matches[i, "end"])
    context <- substr(text, start, end)
    
    # check 30 chars before keyword for negation
    pre_window <- substr(text, max(1, matches[i, "start"] - 30), matches[i, "start"] - 1)
    is_negated <- stringr::str_detect(pre_window, stringr::regex("not|non", ignore_case = TRUE))
    
    if (is_negated) return(NULL)
    
    list(keyword = keyword, context = context)
  })
  
  purrr::compact(results)
}

pattern <- paste(prereg_keywords, collapse = "|")

fulltext_data <- fulltext_data |>
  mutate(
    prereg_context = purrr::map(fulltext, ~ extract_context(.x, pattern))
  )

# extract OSF links (we only want articles that might be preregistered using OSF's template)

fulltext_filtered <- fulltext_data |>
  filter(stringr::str_detect(fulltext, stringr::regex("osf\\.io", ignore_case = TRUE))) |>
   mutate(
    osf_links = stringr::str_extract_all(
      fulltext_data$fulltext[match(uid, fulltext_data$uid)], 
      stringr::regex("osf\\.io/\\S+", ignore_case = TRUE)
    ),
    osf_links = purrr::map_chr(osf_links, ~ paste(.x, collapse = " ||| "))
  ) |>
  mutate(prereg_found = purrr::map_lgl(prereg_context, ~ length(.x) > 0)) |>
  filter(prereg_found) |>
  mutate(
    prereg_context_text = purrr::map(prereg_context, function(contexts) {
      purrr::map_chr(contexts, ~ .x$context) |> paste(collapse = " ||| ")
    }),
    prereg_keyword_matched = purrr::map(prereg_context, function(contexts) {
      purrr::map_chr(contexts, ~ .x$keyword) |> paste(collapse = " ||| ")
    })
  ) |>
  select(-fulltext, -prereg_context) |>
  mutate(prereg_context_text = as.character(prereg_context_text),
         prereg_keyword_matched = as.character(prereg_keyword_matched))

fulltext_filtered <- fulltext_filtered |>
  mutate(
    n_matches = stringr::str_count(prereg_keyword_matched, "\\|\\|\\|") + 1
  )

# add doi
fulltext_filtered <- fulltext_filtered |>
  left_join(data_key |> select(UID, DOI), by = c("uid" = "UID")) %>% 
  # add full text
  left_join(fulltext_data |> select(fulltext, uid), by = "uid")

write.csv(fulltext_filtered, file = "fulltext_filtered.csv")
```
